summary: Improved P2-12 install/new-PC/release equivalence after adding semantic RAG. The Windows installer now runs semantic RAG prewarm by default after build/index: npm run rag:proof and npm run eval:rag execute with DINOBRAIN_REQUIRE_SEMANTIC_EMBEDDINGS=1 and validate that the proof is healthy, uses a real semantic embedding provider rather than local_text_hashing_v1, has zero lexical fallback in eval, and generated_answer_eval is healthy. Added -SkipSemanticRagPrewarm as an explicit opt-out. Added installer semantic RAG verification and wired install_new_pc_release_equivalence into verify:goal with version alignment, hook merge, launcher, and semantic RAG prewarm checks. verify:goal now shows install_new_pc_release_equivalence=true; overall goal remains partial because real Codex Desktop live pre-response proof still needs a fresh user-created thread event.
decisions: Installer default is fail-closed for semantic RAG prewarm by requiring DINOBRAIN_REQUIRE_SEMANTIC_EMBEDDINGS=1.
Local text hashing fallback is explicitly rejected by installer verification instead of being accepted as semantic retrieval.
-SkipSemanticRagPrewarm exists as an explicit operator opt-out, not as silent degrade.
verify:goal now includes install_new_pc_release_equivalence evidence across version alignment, hook merge, launchers, and semantic RAG prewarm.
Do not mark the full goal complete until fresh Codex Desktop live pre-response proof exists.
next_steps: Run npm run codex:live-proof, approve the hook if prompted, paste the proof prompt into a fresh Codex Desktop workspace thread, and rerun npm run verify:goal.
After live proof passes, rerun the full completion audit before marking the goal complete.
